---
title: Importing tokens
description: Import color tokens from CSS variables into Subframe.
---

Import color tokens from existing design systems to match your brand. Subframe supports CSS variable import for colors—typography, borders, corners, and shadows require manual configuration.

## Import locations

Import colors to two locations:

**Individual tokens:** Click **Import** button in the non-palette colors section. Creates standalone color tokens.

**Custom palettes:** Click the import icon on any custom palette. Adds colors to that palette with auto-generated suffixes.

<Note>Default palettes (Brand, Neutral, Error, Success, Warning) don't support import. Create a custom palette first.</Note>

## Supported CSS format

Paste CSS color variables, one per line:

```css
--color-primary-100: #eeeeee;
--color-primary-200: #dddddd;
--color-primary-300: #cccccc;
```

**Format requirements:**
- Must start with `--`
- Color value must be valid hex, rgb, or hsl
- Semicolons optional
- Non-color variables are ignored

Variable names auto-convert to Title Case: `--color-primary-100` becomes "Color Primary 100".

## Import workflow

**Step 1: Paste variables**

Dialog shows textarea with example format. Paste your CSS variables.

**Step 2: Review and edit**

For each detected token:
- Edit palette name (applies to all tokens when importing to palette)
- Edit individual token names
- Adjust color values via color picker

**Step 3: Import**

Click **Import** to add tokens. They appear immediately in your theme.

## After importing

**Sync to code:** Run `npx @subframe/cli sync --all` to update your Tailwind configuration.

**Test across components:** Check that imported colors work with your components and have proper contrast.

## Exporting from design tools

Subframe doesn't directly integrate with Figma or other design tools. Export your design tokens to CSS format first:

1. Use a Figma plugin like "Design Tokens" or "Figma Tokens"
2. Export as CSS variables
3. Paste into Subframe import dialog

## Troubleshooting

<AccordionGroup>
<Accordion title="No color tokens detected">

Verify:
- CSS variables start with `--`
- Color values are valid hex, rgb, or hsl
- Format follows: `--name: value;` or `--name: value`
- Remove JavaScript syntax or preprocessor variables

</Accordion>
<Accordion title="Can't import to default palette">

Import only works with custom palettes. Click **New palette** first, then use the import icon on that palette.

</Accordion>
<Accordion title="Palette name or token names empty">

All names must be filled before import. Edit empty names in the preview step, then click **Import**.

</Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>
